user_story: As a project manager, I want to create a new project with a predefined template and initial settings, so that I can quickly start the project lifecycle and allocate resources efficiently.
true_user_story: The START NEW PROJECT function shall allow the user to create a new project.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to create a new project using a guided wizard that pre-populates standard settings, so that I can reduce setup time and ensure consistency across projects.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to provide the new project name.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to start a new project using a customizable template that includes predefined milestones, task categories, and initial stakeholder assignments, so that I can quickly set up the project structure and ensure consistency across all new initiatives.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to provide the low level document path.

user_story: As a Project Manager, I want to create a new project through a guided wizard that pre‑populates standard settings and templates, so that I can launch the project quickly and ensure consistency across all new initiatives.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to provide the high level document.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to start a new project, so that I can begin planning, allocate resources, and track progress from the outset.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to select an IR Method from the following list: Vector-Space Retrieval Keyword Extraction Keyword Extraction-IDF

user_story: As a project manager, I want to create a new project in the system using a predefined template and initial settings, so that I can quickly start the project with consistent configuration and reduce setup time.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to select a Vocabulary Base of either “Both Documents” or “Low Level Document Only”.  The default value is “Both Documents”.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to create a new project using a guided wizard that sets up the repository, CI/CD pipeline, and baseline documentation, so that my team can start development quickly and consistently without manual configuration.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide Weight Option from the following list: TF-IDF (Default) Okapi LTU

user_story: As a project manager, I want to create a new project using a wizard that pre‑populates standard templates and settings, so that I can launch the project quickly and maintain consistency across the team.
true_user_story: If the user has selected an IR Method of Keyword Extraction or Keyword Extraction-IDF, the user must also select a Weight Threshold.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to create a new project in the system, so that I can set its details, assign resources, and begin tracking its progress.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide a Thesaurus Filename.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to create a new project from a predefined template, so that I can quickly set up project settings, resources, and timelines without starting from scratch.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide the Stop Word file.

user_story: As a project manager, I want a project‑creation wizard that guides me through setting up project details, repository, CI/CD pipelines, and team assignments, so that I can launch new projects quickly and with minimal errors.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide a Feedback Method.  “Standard Rochio” is currently the only option.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to create a new project using a predefined template, so that I can quickly set up the project with consistent settings and start the development process.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to cancel the process and return to the previous state at any time before the project is created.

user_story: As a Project Manager, I want to create a new project with default settings, so that I can quickly set up and launch the project.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to create the project using the above input.  The project shall be placed in “Trace” mode.

user_story: As a developer, I want to load a project from the workspace into the IDE, so that I can quickly start editing, building, and testing the code without manually navigating the file system.
true_user_story: Since the majority of projects are not completed in a single setting, then it is critical to provide the user the option to load an existing project to continue working on the project.  Load Project feature will provide the user the capacity to open an existing project in the last saved state with all the associated mode options. [1]

user_story: As a project manager, I want to load a project from the local file system into the application, so that I can view and edit its tasks, resources, and progress.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to select a project name from a list of saved projects.  Project names are obtained by scanning the saved projects directory.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to load a previously saved project into the system, so that I can resume work on it without having to recreate all the settings and data from scratch.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to select a feedback method.  The only option is “Standard Rochio”.

user_story: As a developer, I want to load a project from my local file system into the IDE, so that I can view, edit, and build the project’s code and resources.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to cancel the LOAD PROJECT action and return to the previous activity.

user_story: As a developer, I want to load a project from the local file system into the IDE, so that I can edit, build, and run the code.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to load the project once the project name and feedback method is selected.

user_story: As a data engineer, I want to load RTM (Real‑Time Metrics) into the data warehouse, so that the analytics team can run real‑time dashboards and alerts on the latest operational data.
true_user_story: Similar in functionality to the START NEW PROJECT function, the LOAD RTM function collects project parameters from the user and initializes the project.  A project created via the LOAD RTM function may be in either “trace” or “assess” mode.  Assess mode is usually used when the user is provided a RTM and needs to evaluate its accuracy, completeness, and satisfaction level.

user_story: As a DevOps engineer, I want to load RTM (Real‑Time Monitoring) data into the monitoring platform, so that I can detect and respond to system anomalies in real time.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to provide the new project name.

user_story: As a data engineer, I want to load RTM data into the data lake, so that the analytics team can run real-time queries and generate timely insights.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to provide the low level document path.

user_story: As a Data Engineer, I want to load RTM data into the data lake, so that analysts can access real-time metrics for dashboards.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to provide the high level document path.

user_story: As a network operations engineer, I want to load RTM (Real‑Time Monitoring) data into the monitoring dashboard using the LOAD RTM feature, so that I can view live metrics and quickly detect and resolve network issues.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to provide the RTM file path.

user_story: As a data engineer, I want to load RTM (Real‑Time Monitoring) data into the data warehouse automatically, so that business users can access up‑to‑date metrics without manual intervention.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to select an IR Method from the following list: Vector-Space Retrieval Keyword Extraction Keyword Extraction-IDF

user_story: As a data engineer, I want to load RTM (Real‑Time Metrics) into our data warehouse automatically, so that analysts can query up‑to‑date data for operational dashboards and real‑time decision making.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to select a Vocabulary Base of either “Both Documents” or “Low Level Document Only”.  The default value is “Both Documents”.

user_story: As a system administrator, I want to load RTM data into the monitoring platform, so that I can view real‑time performance metrics and respond quickly to incidents.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide “Weight Option” from the following list: TF-IDF (Default) Okapi LTU

user_story: As a data engineer, I want to load RTM data into the data lake, so that analysts can access real‑time metrics for decision‑making.
true_user_story: If the user has selected an IR Method of “Keyword Extraction” or “Keyword Extraction-IDF”, the user must also select a “Weight Threshold”.

user_story: As a system administrator, I want to load RTM configuration files into the system, so that the real‑time monitoring service can be automatically configured and deployed.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide a “Thesaurus” filename.

user_story: As a data engineer, I want to automate the loading of RTM (Real‑Time Monitoring) data into our data lake, so that real‑time monitoring dashboards can display up‑to‑date metrics without manual intervention.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide the “Stop Word” filename.

user_story: As a data engineer, I want to load RTM (Real‑Time Monitoring) data into the data lake, so that stakeholders can view up‑to‑date metrics in dashboards and perform real‑time analytics.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide a “Feedback Method”.  “Standard Rochio” is currently the only option.

user_story: As a DevOps engineer, I want to load RTM data into the monitoring platform automatically, so that I can have real‑time visibility into system health and respond to incidents faster.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide the “Project Mode” of either “Trace” or “Assess”.  “Trace” is the default mode.

user_story: As a system administrator, I want to load RTM data into the monitoring system, so that I can view real‑time metrics and detect anomalies immediately.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to cancel the process and return to the previous state at any time before the project is created.

user_story: As a data engineer, I want to load RTM data into the data lake, so that downstream analytics can consume real‑time metrics and provide instant insights.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to create the project using the above input.  The project shall be placed in specified “Project Mode”.

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to save my progress in the application, so that I can resume later without losing my work.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to activate the SAVE function to save the current data and status of the project.

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to save my progress, so that I can resume later without losing my work.
true_user_story: When activated RETRO will save all current data and project.

user_story: As a Project Manager, I want to close the current project, so that the project can be archived, resources can be reallocated, and stakeholders receive a final status report.
true_user_story: The CLOSE CURRENT PROJECT Function will allow the user to close a given project without exiting the RETRO application.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to close the current project by clicking a 'Close Project' button, so that the project is archived, all associated tasks are finalized, and resources can be released for new initiatives.
true_user_story: The user shall be able close the current project.

user_story: As a user, I want a clear and accessible exit button in the main menu, so that I can leave the application quickly and safely.
true_user_story: It is necessary to provide a mechanism to exit the RETRO application when the user is finished working with the application.

user_story: As a user, I want a clear exit button that logs me out and saves my session, so that I can leave the application safely and resume later without losing my work.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to exit the application.

user_story: As a developer, I want to view the raw XML payload of API responses directly in the application’s UI, so that I can debug, validate the structure, and ensure the data meets the expected schema.
true_user_story: The user may want to view the current project’s XML file format.  The SHOW XML function will provide the user a display of the current XML file.

user_story: As a developer, I want to view the XML representation of the data returned by the API, so that I can debug and verify the structure.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to view the XML version of the RTM.

user_story: As a business analyst, I want to generate a customizable report from the analytics dashboard, so that I can quickly share actionable insights with stakeholders and support data‑driven decision making.
true_user_story: After a project has completed either a trace or an assess process, then the user will need the capability to review the final results of the process in an easy to read format other than the XML file format that is automatically generated throughout the process.  The GENERATE REPORT function will provide the user a list of reports that the user may view.  The user will have the option to save the generated report or modify the design of the report. [1]

user_story: As a sales manager, I want to generate a monthly sales report, so that I can review performance and adjust strategies.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to generate and save the “RTM report”

user_story: As a data analyst, I want to generate a report on user engagement metrics, so that I can identify trends and recommend improvements.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to generate and save the “Parent with no children report”

user_story: As a data analyst, I want to generate a customizable report in PDF and Excel formats from the dashboard, so that I can present findings to stakeholders in a clear, shareable format.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to generate and save the “Parents with no children with text report”

user_story: As a business analyst, I want to generate customizable reports from the analytics dashboard, so that I can export key metrics and share actionable insights with stakeholders in a format that suits their needs.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to generate and save the “Children with no parents report”

user_story: As a business analyst, I want to generate a customizable report from the dashboard, so that I can provide stakeholders with up‑to‑date metrics and insights tailored to their needs.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to generate and save the “Children with no parents with text report”

user_story: As a business analyst, I want to generate a quarterly financial report, so that I can present insights to senior management.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to generate and save the “Not satisfied requirements report”

user_story: As a business analyst, I want to generate a customizable sales report with filters for date range, product category, and region, so that I can quickly produce accurate insights for stakeholder meetings.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to generate and save the “Not satisfied requirements with text report”

user_story: As a Requirements Analyst, I want to filter low‑level requirements by status, priority, and component, so that I can quickly identify which requirements need review or are ready for implementation, reducing the time spent sifting through irrelevant items.
true_user_story: “The user shall be able to limit the display of low-level requirements or candidate requirement links.” [1]

user_story: As a Requirements Analyst, I want to filter low‑level requirements by priority and status, so that I can focus on the most critical items and keep the backlog manageable.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to designate whether the filter process applies to the selected high level document element (LOCAL) or to all high level document elements (GLOBAL).

user_story: As a Requirements Engineer, I want to filter low‑level requirements by priority, status, and component, so that I can quickly identify the items that need immediate attention and reduce the noise from lower‑priority or irrelevant requirements.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to filter the list of low-level requirements by setting a cutoff point for the weight between 0 and 1.0.

user_story: As a Requirements Analyst, I want to filter low-level requirements by priority, status, and component, so that I can quickly identify the most critical and relevant items for the current sprint planning.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to designate that the top N elements to be displayed.

user_story: As a Requirements Analyst, I want to filter low‑level requirements by status, priority, and owner, so that I can quickly identify which low‑level items need immediate attention and keep the backlog focused on high‑level priorities.
true_user_story: The user shall be able activate a KILL HIDDEN Links function to remove all elements not currently displayed.

user_story: As a developer, I want to display low‑level elements such as memory addresses, CPU registers, and system logs in the debugging interface, so that I can pinpoint and resolve hardware‑level bugs more efficiently.
true_user_story: The user will need the ability to view the text of low-level requirements.  The DISPLAY function will provide three different sort options to view the text of low-level requirements.  The user can sort the low-level requirements based upon the weight of linking relationships, original document order, or by requirement id. [1]

user_story: As a developer, I want to display low‑level elements in the UI, so that I can debug and verify the underlying data structures.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to sort the low level documents by “Requirement ID”

user_story: As a developer, I want a debug view that displays low‑level elements of the application, so that I can trace data flow, identify performance bottlenecks, and ensure correct component interactions.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to sort the low level documents by “Weights”

user_story: As a developer, I want the debugging interface to display low‑level elements such as stack traces, memory addresses, and system call logs, so that I can pinpoint issues in the code more quickly.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to sort the low level documents by “Document Order”

user_story: As a user, I want to submit feedback through a simple in‑app form, so that my experience can be improved and my concerns are addressed quickly.
true_user_story: Since RETRO’s tracing and assessing modes are based upon algorithms that will allow processes to be improved as the system receives FEEDBACK from the user.

user_story: As a product manager, I want to collect and analyze user feedback through in-app surveys and feedback widgets, so that I can identify pain points and prioritize product roadmap decisions.
true_user_story: The user shall select a low level requirement and identify the link status from the following list: Link No Link Default

user_story: As a project manager, I want a consolidated dashboard that displays the status, deadlines, and resource allocation for all my projects in one place, so that I can quickly identify bottlenecks and reallocate resources without having to open each project individually.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to load multiple projects at the same time.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to view and manage multiple projects from a single dashboard, so that I can efficiently track progress and allocate resources across all initiatives.
true_user_story: The RETRO System shall allow multiple projects to be open at the same time

user_story: As a project manager, I want to import project data from an Excel file into the new system, so that I can quickly set up the project without manually re‑entering all the details.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to import projects.

user_story: As a data engineer, I want to import data from multiple external sources (CSV, JSON, APIs) into our data warehouse, so that we can maintain a unified dataset for reporting and analytics.
true_user_story: The RETRO System shall provide a mechanism to convert existing input text files into RETRO file format.  (Not implemented.)

user_story: As a user, I want a clean, intuitive GUI interface, so that I can navigate the application and complete tasks quickly without confusion.
true_user_story: The user shall use a GUI INTERFACE to work with RETRO.

user_story: As a user, I want a clean, responsive GUI interface, so that I can navigate the application quickly and reduce cognitive load.
true_user_story: The RETRO System shall provide a GUI mechanism for the user to interact with the RETRO System.

user_story: As a user, I want to search for items using a keyword search, so that I can quickly find the content or products I’m looking for.
true_user_story: The user is provided the capability via BROWSE mode to manually link low-level requirements to high-level requirement. In order to help the user to quickly locate the desired low-level requirement or high-level requirement, a KEYWORD SEARCH function is offered to the user. [1]

user_story: As a user, I want to search for content using keywords, so that I can quickly find relevant information.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to enter a keyword into a search text box to search within low-level requirements and the system shall return matches.

user_story: As a user, I want to search for content using keyword search, so that I can quickly find relevant information.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to enter a keyword into a search text box to search within high-level requirements and the system shall return matches.

user_story: As a data engineer, I want to manually link and trace data records across our ETL pipelines, so that I can verify data lineage, troubleshoot data quality issues, and satisfy audit requirements.
true_user_story: There will be times when the user will not agree with RETRO’s list of candidate links.  Therefore, it is important to provide a mechanism for the user to manually change, create, or delete links between low and high level requirements.  BROWSE mode is the mechanism within RETRO that enables the user to perform MANUAL LINKING/TRACING. [1]

user_story: As a data steward, I want to manually link related data entities and trace their lineage, so that I can ensure data quality, compliance, and accurate reporting across the organization.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to create and remove low-level requirements with high-level requirements.

user_story: As a backend engineer, I want to implement distributed tracing across all microservices, so that I can quickly pinpoint latency issues and failures in production.
true_user_story: One of the purposes of RETRO is to aid the user in linking low-level requirements with high-level requirements.  TRACING functionality is the component of RETRO that automatically generates a list of candidate links of high to low-level requirements.

user_story: As a backend engineer, I want to implement distributed tracing across all microservices, so that I can quickly identify latency issues and troubleshoot failures.
true_user_story: RETRO shall provide a mechanism for the user to generate a trace on all requirements.

user_story: As a backend engineer, I want to implement distributed tracing across all microservices, so that I can visualize request flows, pinpoint latency hotspots, and quickly identify failures in production.
true_user_story: RETRO shall provide a mechanism for the user to generate a trace on a given requirement(s).

user_story: As a data analyst, I want to trace each result back to its source data and every processing step that produced it, so that I can verify the accuracy, reproduce the analysis, and quickly identify any errors or anomalies in the data pipeline.
true_user_story: After the TRACING function is performed, then the results of the TRACING process will need to be made available to the user to view.  The TRACING RESULTS functionality will display a list of all candidate links of high to low-level requirements based on any given filtering criteria.  The user will be allowed to provide feedback on the TRACING RESULTS in order to improve future TRACING.

user_story: As a data scientist, I want to trace the results of my machine learning experiments back to the exact dataset, preprocessing steps, and model hyperparameters, so that I can reproduce results and provide evidence for model validation.
true_user_story: RETRO shall display the list of candidate links based on the tracing and permit feedback, filtering, and display functionality.

user_story: As a system administrator, I want the application to automatically save trace logs to a secure, searchable storage, so that I can quickly investigate and resolve incidents, ensuring compliance and system reliability.
true_user_story: The SAVE TRACE function shall allow the user to save the trace result.

user_story: As a developer, I want the application to automatically capture and persist trace logs for each request, so that I can review them later to diagnose failures and performance issues.
true_user_story: RETRO shall provide a mechanism to save the current status of trace.

user_story: As a Supply‑Chain Analyst, I want to complete the trace of each product batch from raw material sourcing through manufacturing, distribution, and retail, so that I can quickly identify and isolate any defective or contaminated batch for recall and ensure full regulatory compliance.
true_user_story: The COMPLETING TRACE function shall allow user to finish the tracing process.

user_story: As a developer, I want the application to automatically generate a complete trace for each request, so that I can quickly identify and resolve bugs.
true_user_story: RETRO shall mark the project completed and not permit further changes.

user_story: As a data engineer, I want to run automated accuracy and completeness checks on our ETL pipelines, so that we can detect and correct data quality issues before the data reaches downstream analytics and reporting systems.
true_user_story: The user needs a mechanism that will help to determine whether the TRACING was successfully performed based on accuracy and completeness.  An accuracy test tells the user that the candidate links provided are correctly related to the high-level requirement.  Completeness test tells the user that all possible links that are listed in the candidate list are included and no low-level requirements are missing. [1]

user_story: As a QA lead, I want the run execution system to automatically verify that each test run produces accurate and complete results, so that we can trust the build quality and reduce manual verification time.
true_user_story: RETRO shall provide a mechanism for the user to be able to run an ACCURACY & COMPLETENESS check by performing a full trace on the requirements and comparing the IR method results to the given RTM. [1]

user_story: As a user, I want to view a list of personalized recommendations, so that I can quickly discover content that matches my interests and preferences.
true_user_story: “The [user] will need to have a list of recommendations based on RETRO’s TRACING process.  SHOW RECOMMENDATION will display to the [user] a list of recommendation while in ASSESS mode.  The [user] will have the capability to provide FEEDBACK to the RETRO system on whether or not the [user] agrees with RETRO’s assessment of the linking between the low-level requirements to the high-level requirements.” [1]

user_story: As a logged‑in user, I want to see a dynamic list of personalized recommendations displayed on my dashboard, so that I can quickly discover new content or products that match my interests without having to search manually.
true_user_story: RETRO shall display/hide recommendations at the user’s request.

user_story: As a Customer Success Analyst, I want to run a satisfaction analysis on the latest survey data, so that I can identify areas for improvement and increase customer retention.
true_user_story: Satisfaction function shows how satisfied the high-level requirement is with the given set of low-level requirements. [4] The Satisfaction Analysis function is checking the level of percentage for a satisfaction of the linked result with the high and low level input files. Once final output is agreed, the list of high level requirement is updated with options like “Satisfied”, “Not Satisfied” and “Partially Satisfied”. The status of high level requirement is color coded. Unsatisfied result can be repeated until all the high levels has been satisfied stage.

user_story: As a Customer Success Manager, I want to run a satisfaction analysis on our latest survey data, so that I can identify trends, pinpoint pain points, and proactively improve the customer experience.
true_user_story: RETRO shall display to the user the percentage level of link. [1]

user_story: As a content manager, I want to remove all items that have no links, so that the content library remains high‑quality and users can easily find relevant resources.
true_user_story: While in ASSESS mode, the user will need a mechanism to allow the removal of all low-level requirements that are not linked to a high-level requirement.  REMOVE ALL NO LINKS function will provide an easy way for the user to quickly remove low-level requirements with status of “No Link”.

user_story: As a content manager, I want to automatically remove all items that have no link (i.e., null or empty link fields), so that the website’s navigation and content lists remain clean and users don’t encounter empty or broken links.
true_user_story: RETRO shall remove all links marked as “No Link”

user_story: As a content curator, I want to delete recommended links from the recommendation list, so that the recommendations remain relevant and accurate for users.
true_user_story: While in ASSESS mode, the user will need a mechanism that will allow the deletion of recommended links that the user feels are not correctly linked.

user_story: As a content manager, I want to delete recommended links from the recommendation engine, so that the list remains fresh and relevant for users.
true_user_story: RETRO shall permit the user to select a recommended link and delete it.

user_story: As a customer support agent, I want to see color‑coded satisfaction status indicators on each ticket, so that I can quickly identify and prioritize tickets based on customer sentiment.
true_user_story: “In order to help the user to quickly identify the level of satisfaction of each candidate link, RETRO will need to provide a visual aid.  COLOR-CODED SATISFACTION STATUS function will color code each candidate link based on its satisfaction level.   The various colors will be a visual aid to the user to help quickly identify each links satisfaction level.” [1]

user_story: As a customer support agent, I want each support ticket to display a color‑coded satisfaction status (e.g., green for satisfied, yellow for neutral, red for dissatisfied), so that I can instantly identify which tickets require urgent follow‑up and which are already resolved satisfactorily.
true_user_story: RETRO shall COLOR-CODE THE SATISFACTION STATUS of each given candidate link.
